name: Evelyn Hart
household: Lives alone in a small single-family home; daughter (Melissa) lives ~20 minutes away and checks in weekly
goals: Stay independent at home as long as it’s safe
Build reliable routines for medications, appointments, and bills
Reduce missed appointments by using one calendar system consistently
Keep an active social life (bridge, church, pool) without embarrassment about memory slips
Create a clear plan with family for healthcare decisions and finances
Preserve everyday memories (photos, notes) for her children and grandkids
pain_points: Mixing up days and times, especially when plans change
Misplacing essential items (glasses, keys, paperwork) and wasting energy searching
Feeling overwhelmed by mail/bills and uncertainty about what is real vs. spam/scams
Anxiety spikes when she can’t find words or forgets names in social settings
Sensory overload in busy places (mall, grocery store), leading to confusion and mistakes
Fear of losing independence (especially around driving and finances) and reluctance to ask for help

personality.communication_style: warm and polite, sometimes apologetic; uses gentle humor; can get flustered when rushed
personality.privacy_sensitivity: medium-high — cautious after scam attempts and worried about exposing financial/health details
personality.ai_assistant_tone: calm, reassuring, practical, step-by-step; confirm dates clearly; never talk down to her
strengths: responds well to routines and written checklists
growth_areas: managing anxiety when memory slips happen

ai_tools: None historically; open to a voice-based helper if it feels safe and simple
productivity: Paper notes and fridge calendar (large print)
tech_usage.frequency: Uses phone and email daily, but prefers low-friction tools; tends to do best with one consistent system and visible cues at home
